summary.intent: Address a second round of Codex review findings on PR #732 (ars-ui) after a first-pass fix commit, then retrigger Codex and poll for approval.
summary.outcome: All three new Codex findings (Dioxus hook-order bug, overly broad lint suppressions, CSS :has() focus selector) were fixed, tested, committed as '1912bf776' second-pass fix, pushed, and all review threads resolved. Codex was retriggered for a third pass.

repo: The repo uses a strict lint policy: broad crate-level #[allow(...)] suppressions are not acceptable; findings must use targeted #[expect(...)] on the specific call site.
Dioxus hook-order rule: all hooks (use_store, etc.) must be called unconditionally before any render gate or early return — placing them inside a `can_close` conditional is a real dynamic-store risk.

Both ars-dioxus-components and ars-leptos-components share parallel CSS template structures; a focus-visible selector fix typically needs to be applied in both css.rs and tailwind.rs variants for each framework.
The project uses `cargo xfmt` (not `cargo fmt`) as its formatter command.
WASM tests are run with `WASM_BINDGEN_TEST_ONLY_WEB=1 cargo test -p ars-dioxus --features web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --test tabs_wasm`.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment; active review is indicated by a 👀 reaction and terminal state by its removal.

code.finding: Inline rsx event handler syntax (e.g., onclick: |_| {...}) triggers a workspace `unused_qualifications` lint; the fix is to move the handler to a local closure variable before passing it in.
CloseTrigger component was calling hooks conditionally inside a can_close render gate; hooks must be hoisted above the gate to comply with Dioxus hook ordering rules.
CSS :has() focus-visible selector was not using the mirrored shell state attribute; should reference the shell attribute directly rather than a derived :has() expression.

workflow: The review loop pattern: fetch unresolved Codex threads via GraphQL, fix each, commit, push, reply to each thread with the fix commit SHA via gh API, resolve threads via GraphQL mutation, post @codex review, then poll at 5-minute intervals checking reactions and unresolved thread count.
Polling state is determined by two signals: presence of 👀 reaction (reviewing in progress) and unresolved Codex thread count (new findings when > 0 after 👀 drops).
When a wasm test emits compiler warnings, they must be cleaned up before committing — the project enforces warning-free builds.
Running `cargo check -p <crate>` after removing lint suppressions is the right gate before committing to catch any fallout.

friction: Dioxus rsx inline event handler syntax unconditionally triggers `unused_qualifications` workspace lint, requiring a workaround (local closure) even in test code.
The WASM test target requires an environment variable prefix (`WASM_BINDGEN_TEST_ONLY_WEB=1`) that is easy to miss and not obvious from the test output.
Codex takes 25-30 minutes per review pass, requiring a long polling loop before results are available.
open_items: Third Codex review pass was in progress at transcript end — outcome (approval or further findings) is unknown.
The CSS :has() focus selector fix was applied but no dedicated CSS regression test was added to lock it in; a future test could verify the selector renders correctly in both adapters.
The hook-order fix in CloseTrigger was validated with a single wasm test probe; broader coverage of dynamic closability toggling with custom shell renderers may still be missing.
